original_hypothesis.title: THORChain_Router.transferAllowance External Router Path Creates Stale Token Approvals
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When transferAllowance is called with router != address(this), _routerDeposit approves the external router for tokens and calls its depositWithExpiry. If the external router's depositWithExpiry succeeds but does not actually pull tokens via transferFrom (e.g., a malicious contract that accepts the call but does nothing), the approval remains active. The external address retains permanent approval to pull tokens from the THORChain_Router at any future time.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The _routerDeposit flow: (1) decrement vault allowance, (2) approve external router, (3) call depositWithExpiry. If step 3 succeeds without pulling tokens, the approval from step 2 persists. The external address could later call transferFrom to drain tokens from the Router.

proof_of_signal.pattern: safeApprove(asset, router, amount) then iROUTER(router).depositWithExpiry(); no check that approval was consumed

preconditions: THORChain nodes specify an external router that does not pull tokens during depositWithExpiry
Or node software bug causes use of a non-conformant external router address
External address later calls token.transferFrom(THORChain_Router, attacker, amount)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: safeApprove grants approval; no verification that approval was consumed post-depositWithExpiry
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Stale approval allows external address to drain tokens at any future time
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker gains access to token amount equal to the stale approval

economic_questions: Can THORChain consensus be tricked into specifying a malicious external router?
Is there a mechanism to revoke stale approvals?
What is the maximum value exposed via a single stale approval?
validation_requirements: Deploy mock external router that doesn't pull tokens
Confirm external address can later transferFrom the Router

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time after stale approval is created

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All vaults with allowances for the affected token
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Full token balance of the Router for that asset

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: How external router addresses are validated in THORChain consensus

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
